name: Cesar (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the fertile valley of the Cesar river and the cattle-ranching plains of the department
the perpetual snows of the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta and the peace of the Cesar people

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of green, white, and green.
history: Cesar was created as a department on 21 December 1967, separating from Magdalena. Its flag's green-white-green tricolour evokes the contrast between the green Cesar valley and the snowy peaks of the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta that overlook it.

features.symbolism: green for the fertility of the Cesar valley and the foothills of the Sierra Nevada; white for the snowy peaks of the Sierra Nevada de Santa Marta and the peace of its people
